message:
fix(func): sign() rejects NaN/Infinity text and avoids double-parse

Rust's f64::parse accepts "NaN", "inf", "Infinity", "-inf" etc. as
valid floats, but C SQLite's sqlite3AtoF does not recognise these
strings as numeric — sign() must return NULL for them.

Changes:
- In the Text arm of SignFunc::invoke, use `if let Ok(f) = parse`
  instead of a separate is_ok() check followed by args[0].to_float().
  This eliminates the redundant double-parse and uses the already-
  parsed f64 value directly.
- After a successful f64 parse, reject NaN and infinite values by
  returning NULL, matching C SQLite behaviour.
- Add unit test test_sign_nan_inf_text_returns_null covering "NaN",
  "nan", "inf", "-inf", "Infinity", "-Infinity", "INF".
- Add conformance oracle test test_conformance_sign_nan_inf_s70f that
  validates against rusqlite (C SQLite) for all these inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
